4.1: Dear friends, do not be credulous about every spiritual claim—test them to see which ones come from God. The reason is urgent: many false prophets have spread through the world.
4.2: Here is how to recognize the Spirit of God: every spirit that openly confesses Jesus Christ as truly come in human flesh is from God,
4.3: but any spirit that refuses to confess Jesus is not from God—it is the spirit of antichrist. You have been warned this spirit is coming, and it is already at work in the world right now.
4.4: But you, dear children, are from God, and you have conquered these spirits—because the One living inside you is far more powerful than the one at work in the world.
4.5: These false prophets belong to the world, so they speak its language, and the world recognizes its own voice and listens.
4.6: We are from God. Anyone who truly knows God will hear our message; anyone who is not from God will not. This is the test that distinguishes the Spirit of truth from the spirit of deception.
4.7: Dear friends, let us love one another with genuine love. Love itself originates in God, and everyone who loves demonstrates that they have been born of God and truly know him.
4.8: But anyone who does not love has not truly known God at all—for God is love. That is his very nature.
4.9: And here is how God's love became visible and concrete: he sent his unique, beloved Son into the world so that through him we might have life.
4.10: And here is the true definition of love—not that we initiated anything toward God, but that he first loved us and sent his Son to deal with our sins through his atoning death.
4.11: Dear friends, if God loved us with that kind of love, then loving one another is not optional—it is the only fitting response.
4.12: No human eye has ever seen God directly; but when we love one another, God lives in us and his love reaches its full, intended purpose in us.
4.13: Here is how we know we dwell in him and he in us: he has shared his Spirit with us.
4.14: We are eyewitnesses to this, and we are declaring it publicly: the Father sent his Son to be the Savior of the world.
4.15: Whoever publicly declares that Jesus is the Son of God—God lives in that person, and that person lives in God.
4.16: We have come to know, and to rest our whole weight upon, the love God has for us. The conclusion stands: God is love—and whoever lives in love lives in God, and God lives in them.
4.17: And here is love's goal brought to completion: we stand before the judgment seat with boldness—not because we are innocent, but because we are united to him, and as he is, so are we in this world.
4.18: Fear has no place where love is fully at home. Perfect love expels fear—because fear is always anticipating punishment. Anyone still caught in fear has not yet been brought to maturity in love.
4.19: We are only capable of loving at all because he loved us first.
4.20: Anyone who claims to love God while hating a fellow believer is lying to themselves. You cannot love the God you have never seen while refusing to love the brother or sister standing right in front of you.
4.21: The command from God himself settles it: whoever loves God must love their brothers and sisters as well.

5.1: Every person who believes that Jesus is the Messiah has been born of God. And this follows: if you love the Father, you will also love his children.
5.2: Here is the proof that we love God's children: we love God himself and we actually keep his commands.
5.3: For what does it actually mean to love God? It means keeping his commands. And those commands are not crushing—they do not weigh us down.
5.4: Because every child of God wins victory over the world. And what is the power that wins this victory? Our faith.
5.5: And who exactly is this overcomer? Only the person who genuinely believes that Jesus is the Son of God.
5.6: Jesus Christ—this is the one whose coming was marked by the water of his baptism and the blood of his death. Not water only, but water and blood together. And the Spirit continues to testify about him, because the Spirit is truth itself.
5.7: There are three witnesses to this:
5.8: the Spirit, the water, and the blood—and these three witnesses say the same thing.
5.9: We routinely accept human testimony in daily life. But God's testimony carries infinitely greater weight—and this is precisely what he has testified: the truth about his Son.
5.10: The person who trusts in the Son of God carries this testimony within themselves—they have internalized it. But anyone who refuses to believe is effectively calling God a liar, since they are rejecting God's own testimony about his Son.
5.11: And here is what God's testimony says: he has given us age-to-come life—resurrection life, genuine and full—and this life exists only in his Son.
5.12: Have the Son, and you have life. Reject the Son of God, and life is simply not yours.
5.13: I am writing all of this to you who trust in the name of the Son of God—so that you can know with certainty, not merely hope, that you have this eternal life.
5.14: And this is what that confidence looks like in practice: whenever we pray for anything that aligns with his will, he hears us—truly, attentively hears us.
5.15: If we know he is actually listening to every request we bring, then we can be certain: whatever we have asked for according to his will, it has been granted.
5.16: If you see a fellow believer committing a sin that does not lead to death, pray for them—and God will grant life to that person. This applies to sins not leading to death. There is such a thing as a sin that leads to death—for that I am not directing you to pray.
5.17: Every act of unrighteousness is sin—that is clear. But not every sin is the kind that leads to death.
5.18: We know this: every person born of God does not live a life dominated by sin. The Son of God—himself born of God—guards them, and the evil one cannot lay hold of them.
5.19: We know that we belong to God—and we also know that the whole world system lies under the power of the evil one.
5.20: And we know that the Son of God has come—he is here—and he has given us the capacity to understand, so that we can know the One who is truly real. We live in the Real One, in his Son Jesus Christ. He—this Jesus Christ—is himself the true God and is eternal life.
5.21: My dear children, guard yourselves from every substitute for God.

1.1: We are writing about something that has existed from the very beginning—something we have heard with our own ears, seen with our own eyes, gazed upon and touched with our own hands. This is our announcement: the Word who is life itself.
1.2: This life actually broke into our world—we saw it with our own eyes and we bear witness to it. We now announce to you the eternal life that was with the Father from the beginning and was revealed to us in person.
1.3: We announce to you what we have seen and heard, so that you can share in the same life we share—a life of deep communion with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
1.4: We are writing this so that our joy may be full to overflowing.
1.5: Here is the message we received from him and now pass on to you: God is light—pure, undimmed light—and in him there is no darkness whatsoever.
1.6: If we claim to be in fellowship with God but keep living in darkness, we are lying—we are not living according to the truth.
1.7: But if we are walking in the light—as God himself is in the light—then we have genuine fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus his Son keeps purifying us from every sin.
1.8: If we tell ourselves we have no sin, we are only deceiving ourselves—the truth has no place in us.
1.9: But if we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous—he will forgive us our sins and cleanse us from every form of wrongdoing.
1.10: If we claim we have never sinned, we are calling God a liar, and his word has no home in our lives.

2.1: My dear children, I am writing this so that you will not sin. But when someone does sin, we have a defender who stands before the Father on our behalf—Jesus Christ, the Righteous One.
2.2: He is the sacrifice that turns away God's wrath—not only for our sins but for the sins of the entire world.
2.3: Here is how we know we truly know him: we keep his commands.
2.4: Anyone who claims to know God but ignores his commands is a liar—the truth has no home in them.
2.5: But when someone keeps his word, God's love reaches its full expression in their life. This is how we know we are truly united with him:
2.6: Anyone who claims to live in union with God must walk the same path Jesus walked.
2.7: Dear friends, what I am writing is not a new command—it is the ancient one you have known from the start. This old command is simply the message you heard from the beginning.
2.8: And yet in another sense it is a new command—one already proving itself true in Christ and in you, because the old darkness is fading and the true light is already breaking through.
2.9: Whoever says they are in the light while hating a fellow believer is still in the dark—nothing has changed for them.
2.10: But whoever loves their fellow believer is living in the light, and there is nothing in them to cause another to fall.
2.11: Anyone who hates a fellow believer is in the dark and walks in the dark—they don't even know where they are going, because the darkness has robbed them of their sight.
2.12: I write to you, dear children, because your sins have already been forgiven—for the sake of his name.
2.13: I write to you, fathers, because you have come to know the One who has been from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you have conquered the evil one. I write to you, children, because you know the Father.
2.14: I have written to you, fathers, because you have known the One who is from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, because you are strong—God's word has taken deep root in you, and you have overcome the evil one.
2.15: Do not set your affections on the world or on what the world offers. If anyone is in love with the world, love for the Father has no place in them.
2.16: Everything the world offers—the craving of the body, the craving of the eyes, the arrogant display of wealth and status—none of it comes from the Father. It all belongs to the world.
2.17: The world and all its cravings are fading away—but whoever does God's will stands firm forever.
2.18: Dear children, we are in the final hour. You heard that the antichrist was coming—and sure enough, many antichrists have already arrived. This is how we know we are living in the end times.
2.19: These people came from our community, but they never truly belonged to it. If they had really been part of us, they would have stayed. Their departure makes plain that none of them were genuinely ours.
2.20: But you have received an anointing from the Holy One—and all of you have knowledge of the truth.
2.21: I am not writing because you are ignorant of the truth—you know it well. I write because no lie can ever spring from the truth.
2.22: Who is the supreme liar? The one who denies that Jesus is the Christ. That is the antichrist—the one who refuses to acknowledge the Father and the Son.
2.23: Deny the Son and you forfeit the Father. Confess the Son and you have the Father as well.
2.24: Hold firmly to what you heard from the beginning—let it take deep root in you. If the original message stays in you, you will remain in union with the Son and with the Father.
2.25: And here is the promise he himself made to us: life that is eternal—the life of the age to come.
2.26: I write all this to warn you about those who are trying to lead you away from the truth.
2.27: But the anointing you received from him lives in you, and you don't need anyone else to teach you. His anointing teaches you about everything—it is genuine, not a counterfeit—so as it has taught you, stay in union with him.
2.28: And so, dear children, stay in union with him—so that when he appears, we will stand before him with confidence and will not cringe in shame at his arrival.
2.29: You know that he is righteous—so know this too: everyone who lives righteously has been born of him.

3.1: Look at the lavish love the Father has poured out on us—that we should actually be called God's children. And that is exactly what we are! The world does not recognize us, because it never recognized him.
3.2: Dear friends, we are already God's children—though what we will become has not yet been fully revealed. But we know this: when he appears, we will be like him, because we will see him as he truly is.
3.3: Anyone who holds this hope fixed on him will keep purifying themselves—because he himself is pure.
3.4: Whoever keeps on sinning is in open rebellion against God's law—that is what sin is: defiance of God's order.
3.5: You know why he appeared: to take sins away. And in him there is absolutely no sin.
3.6: Whoever stays in union with him does not keep on sinning. Anyone who keeps on sinning has never truly seen him or known him.
3.7: Dear children, don't let anyone mislead you: the person who actually lives righteously is righteous—just as he is righteous.
3.8: Whoever keeps sinning belongs to the devil—the devil has been sinning since the beginning. But the Son of God appeared for this very purpose: to dismantle everything the devil has built.
3.9: Those who have been born of God do not persist in sinning—because God's own life-seed lives in them. They cannot keep on sinning, because they have been born of God.
3.10: This is the clear dividing line between God's children and the devil's children: anyone who does not live righteously does not belong to God—and neither does anyone who does not love their fellow believers.
3.11: The message has been the same from the very start: we are to love one another.
3.12: Unlike Cain, who belonged to the evil one and killed his brother—why did he kill him? Because Cain's actions were evil and his brother's were righteous. Righteousness provokes hatred in those who have none.
3.13: So do not be shocked, dear brothers and sisters, when the world hates you.
3.14: We know that we have crossed over from death into life—because we love our fellow believers. Anyone who does not love is still living in death.
3.15: Whoever hates a fellow believer is, in God's sight, a murderer—and you know that no murderer has the life of the age to come living in them.
3.16: This is where we learn what love truly is: he laid down his life for us. That means we, too, ought to lay down our lives for one another.
3.17: But if someone has what the world calls abundance, and sees a fellow believer going without, and shuts their heart—how can God's love possibly live in them?
3.18: Dear children, let us not love merely with words or fine-sounding speeches—let us love through what we do and in truth.
3.19: This is how we will know we belong to the truth, and how we will put our hearts at peace before him—
3.20: even when our own hearts condemn us. For God is greater than our hearts, and he knows all things.
3.21: Dear friends, if our hearts do not condemn us, we approach God with complete confidence—
3.22: and receive from him whatever we ask, because we keep his commands and do what is pleasing in his sight.
3.23: And his command comes down to this: believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another—just as he himself commanded.
3.24: Whoever keeps his commands stays in union with God, and God remains in them. And here is how we know that he dwells in us: by the Spirit he has given us.
